name: Magellan's Cross
description: A cross planted by Ferdinand Magellan upon his arrival in Cebu in 1521. Housed in a chapel along Magallanes St, next to the Basilica Minore del Santo Niño.
jeepney_tip: Ride any jeep going to Colon or Carbon. Alight at Magallanes St. Walk 1 minute toward the Basilica.
keywords: magellan cross historical landmark cebu heritage church colonial

name: Basilica Minore del Santo Niño
description: The oldest Roman Catholic church in the Philippines (est. 1565). Home to the venerated image of the Santo Niño de Cebú, patron saint of the city.
jeepney_tip: Ride any jeep going to Colon. Alight at Osmeña Blvd junction. The Basilica is directly visible.
keywords: basilica santo nino oldest church cebu heritage pilgrimage sinulog

name: Fort San Pedro
description: The oldest and smallest fort in the Philippines, built by Miguel López de Legazpi in 1565. Now a heritage museum overlooking Cebu port.
address: A. Pigafetta St, Cebu City (near Pier 1)
jeepney_tip: Ride any jeep to Pier 1 or Carbon. Walk toward the port area — the fort is beside the port gate.
keywords: fort san pedro spanish colonial heritage museum port pier cebu city

name: Temple of Leah
description: A grand Roman-style temple built by Teodorico Adarna for his late wife Leah. Perched on the Busay hills with a breathtaking panoramic view of Cebu City and the sea.
jeepney_tip: Ride jeep 04B or 04D to Lahug terminal. From Lahug, take a habal-habal (motorbike taxi) up Busay Road to the temple — about 15 min ride.
keywords: temple leah busay roman monument love cebu city panoramic view

name: Sirao Flower Garden
description: Known as Cebu's 'Little Amsterdam,' this highland flower farm bursts with celosias, dahlias, and sunflowers. Cool mountain air and stunning terraced views.
jeepney_tip: Ride jeep 04B to Lahug terminal, then take a habal-habal to Sirao. Alternatively, rent a multicab from Lahug for the group.
keywords: sirao garden flowers amsterdam cebu highlands nature instagrammable

name: Tops Lookout
description: The highest accessible viewpoint in Cebu City at 600 meters above sea level. Offers a 360° panorama of the metro, neighboring islands, and the Cebu Strait.
jeepney_tip: Ride jeep 04B to Lahug terminal. Take a habal-habal or multicab to Tops. Best visited just before sunset for golden hour views.
keywords: tops lookout cebu panoramic view mountain busay sunset night view

name: Carbon Market
description: The largest and oldest public market in Cebu, established in 1910. A vibrant sensory explosion of fresh produce, dried fish, local delicacies, flowers, and ukay-ukay (thrift shops).
address: M. C. Briones St, Carbon, Cebu City
jeepney_tip: Carbon is the terminal for many routes — just ask any driver 'Carbon?' and you'll get there. Routes 41D, 42B, 43, 44, and 45 all end here.
keywords: carbon market public market cebu fresh produce dried fish ukay thrift local food

name: Ayala Center Cebu
description: Cebu's premier upscale shopping destination. Home to international brands, The Terraces al fresco dining, an ice skating rink, and a rooftop events space. Also a major jeepney transfer hub.
address: Archbishop Reyes Ave, Lahug, Cebu City
jeepney_tip: Many routes pass through Ayala. Ride 03L, 04L, or 04M from SM City or Lahug. The terminal is at the Ayala Activity Center.
keywords: ayala center cebu mall shopping dining ice skating upscale terraces

name: Cebu IT Park
description: The beating heart of Cebu's BPO and tech industry. By day a bustling business district, by night a vibrant food hub with dozens of restaurants, bars, and cafes open until the early morning.
jeepney_tip: Ride jeep 04I (Lahug–IT Park) or 17B (IT Park–Jones). The jeepney stop is along Salinas Drive, main entrance of IT Park.
keywords: it park cebu bpo tech business district food park nightlife

name: Plaza Independencia
description: A historic park near Fort San Pedro where the Philippine flag was first raised in Cebu. Features monuments, a lighthouse reproduction, and scenic views of Cebu Harbor.
jeepney_tip: Ride any jeep toward Pier or take route 04H (Lahug–Plaza). Alight at Osmeña Blvd near Pier 1. The plaza is a short walk.
keywords: plaza independencia park historical cebu heritage harbor monument flag

name: SM Seaside City Cebu
description: One of the largest malls in the Philippines, built on reclaimed land with a stunning view of the Cebu Strait. Features an outdoor Sky Park, bowling, and an IMAX cinema.
jeepney_tip: The CIBUS modern jeep runs from IT Park → Jones → SM Seaside. It's the most direct route to SRP from the city center.
keywords: sm seaside cebu mall srp south road properties imax sky park

name: Colon Street
description: The oldest street in the Philippines, established in 1565. A bustling commercial strip running through the heart of old Cebu City, lined with shops, markets, and local eateries.
jeepney_tip: Colon is the terminal for dozens of routes. Simply ask any driver heading downtown — almost every route passes through or ends at Colon.
keywords: colon street oldest philippine street cebu downtown commercial heritage

name: Naga Boardwalk
description: A scenic waterfront promenade in the City of Naga with breathtaking sunset views over the Cebu Strait. A peaceful retreat with food stalls, benches, and fresh sea breeze.
jeepney_tip: Ride route 44 (Naga–Cebu City) or route 45 (San Fernando–Cebu City). Alight at Naga Poblacion. The boardwalk is along the shoreline.
keywords: naga boardwalk sunset sea view cebu south promenade nature relax

name: University of San Carlos (USC)
description: One of the oldest universities in Asia (est. 1595 as a colegio). The main campus on P. Del Rosario St is a heritage landmark with beautiful Spanish-era architecture and a natural history museum.
address: P. Del Rosario St, Cebu City
jeepney_tip: Ride jeep 13B, 13C, or 62B from downtown — they all pass USC Main. Alight at Ramos St junction.
keywords: usc university san carlos oldest asia heritage architecture cebu education museum

name: Taoist Temple
description: A serene Chinese Taoist temple tucked in the hills of Beverly Hills subdivision, Cebu. Featuring traditional Chinese architecture, dragons, and panoramic city views.
address: Beverly Hills Subdivision, Nivel Hills, Cebu City
jeepney_tip: Ride jeep 04L toward Lahug. Alight at JY Square then take a habal-habal up to Beverly Hills / Nivel Hills area.
keywords: taoist temple chinese heritage cebu hills panoramic view culture religion
